items.headline: City addresses affordability protections for subsidized housing
items.summary: The Council is reviewing policies to maintain affordability restrictions on housing units if project-based subsidy contracts expire or properties face foreclosure. This affects residents living in subsidized affordable housing developments across San José.

items.headline: City Council reviews FY 2026-27 capital improvement projects
items.summary: The City Council held a study session on April 14 to discuss Mountain View's planned capital improvements for the next fiscal year. This is where major infrastructure and development projects get vetted before formal approval.

items.headline: Sunnyvale updates tenant protection rules
items.summary: The Housing and Human Services Commission introduced an ordinance amending the city's Residential Tenant Protections Program. This could affect renter rights and landlord obligations in Sunnyvale.

items.headline: Library facilities plan and youth services programs reviewed
items.summary: The City Council received status updates on the Library Facilities and Customer Experience Plan, plus annual reports on youth programs including the Safe Summer Initiative. This signals potential improvements to library services and youth programming in San José.

items.headline: Historic Kipling Street building eligible for significant expansion
items.summary: The City Council is considering elevating 405 Kipling Street from a Category 4 to Category 2 historic resource, which would allow developers to add 5,000 square feet of bonus floor area in exchange for historic and seismic rehabilitation work. This could substantially change the building's footprint in downtown Palo Alto.

items.headline: Major mixed-use development proposed on San Antonio Road
items.summary: A developer is proposing to rezone and redevelop 788-790 and 796 San Antonio Road with an 8-story building containing 167 residential units (28 affordable) and 1,400 square feet of commercial space. The project requires rezoning from Service Commercial to a new Planned Community district and will be reviewed by the City Council.

items.headline: Mountain View zoning hearing postponed; 333 Franklin Street project delayed
items.summary: The Administrative Zoning hearing scheduled for April 8 has been cancelled and rescheduled for April 22, 2026. A project at 333 Franklin Street that was set to be reviewed will now be re-noticed with a new hearing date, delaying its consideration by the Planning Commission.

items.headline: City Council approves construction contract with QLM, Inc.
items.summary: The Town Manager was authorized to execute a construction contract with QLM, Inc., though the agenda document does not specify which project this contract covers. Residents interested in upcoming construction should monitor the city website for details on the scope and location of this work.
